Name: 52_energy_conducted_aerosol_weapon
Representative_Docs: ['Aerosol Spray — Oleoresin Capsicum (Pepper) Spray To reduce the risk of unintentional injury, the use of an aerosol spray should be avoided on the following groups of people unless urgent circumstances exist: People believed to be 65 years of age or older. Any person in control of a vehicle that is in gear or in motion. Any person in a position that creates the likelihood of significant additional injury other than those effects reasonably anticipated due to the use of the aerosol spray. Once a subject has been sprayed and subdued and the scene is safe, officers shall monitor the person’s breathing and consciousness. As soon as reasonable, officers shall decontaminate the effected prisoner by flushing the exposed area with fresh water and then exposing the area to fresh air. Emergency Medical Services shall be contacted if the subject who has been sprayed: Appears to be taking an excessive amount of time to recover. Exhibits any signs of respiratory or other distress. Complains of a preexisting respiratory condition. Was wearing contact lenses and/or complains of having a preexisting eye injury or condition. Requests medical treatment. Conducted energy weapons and aerosol sprays shall only be used in situations where the subject poses an immediate and continuing danger or to stop the flight of a subject trying to avoid arrest and presenting a risk to the safety of the community or others if not immediately apprehended. The officer must be reasonably able to articulate the risk of safety. Non-compliance and non-violent physical resistance do not necessarily create an immediate and continuing danger. Conducted energy weapons and aerosol sprays shall not be used on a subject who physically resists in a non-violent manner, unless the situation is such that an officer could reasonably conclude that the resistance presents some immediate danger, despite the subject’s non-violent character.', 'To reduce the risk of unintentional injury, the use of an aerosol spray should be avoided on the following groups of people unless urgent circumstances exist: People believed to be 65 years of age or older. Women reasonably believed to be pregnant. Visibly frail people. Any person in control of a vehicle that is in gear or in motion. Any person’s head, neck or genitalia. Any person in a position that creates the likelihood of significant additional injury other than those effects reasonably anticipated due to the use of the conducted energy weapon. A conducted energy weapon will not be used under the following circumstances: On a person actively being sprayed with any aerosol weapon. In proximity to any known flammable or highly combustible substances that may be ignited by the conducted energy weapon, to include any person who has been exposed to known flammable or highly combustible substances such as gasoline. Removal of conducted energy weapon probes in non-sensitive areas may be done by officers according to probe-removal training guidelines. Officers, or other trained personnel, will provide first aid following the removal of the probes as needed. Medical personnel (such as Emergency Medical Services) shall be used to: Remove probes in sensitive areas. Remove probes that have broken off and are imbedded under the skin. In any situation where officers on the scene believe the use of medical personnel is appropriate. Name: 53_agreements_private_companies_rights
Representative_Docs: ['In most cases and weather permitting, private property and public rights of way should be restored to original condition within three to five business days once construction in an area is complete. Companies are not required by law to coordinate their activities. Once the work of one company is complete, the construction process could start again by another company. Construction in your area does not mean that new or improved service will be available to you immediately. Residents should reach out to the internet service providers directly for availability timelines.', 'The utility companies have the legal right to operate within rights of way of the town and of the North Carolina Department of Transportation. The town can impose additional conditions in its rights of way through encroachment agreements with utilities that do not already have special agreements with the state. For utilities that have special agreements with the state, the town only may require utilities to keep roads functional, follow existing laws and repair any damage caused in the rights of way.', 'Work within Public Rights of Way The utility companies have the legal right to operate within rights of way of the town and of the North Carolina Department of Transportation. The town can impose additional conditions in its rights of way through encroachment agreements with utilities that do not already have special agreements with the state. For utilities that have special agreements with the state, the town only may require utilities to keep roads functional, follow existing laws and repair any damage caused in the rights of way.']

Name: 56_handlers_dogs_jett_canine
Representative_Docs: ['Canine Training Police dogs are certified annually by an independent trainer. Each month, the canine officers and their handlers train for at least 16 hours in a variety of scenarios and in different locations and weather conditions. They also train with handlers and dogs from surrounding agencies.', 'Canine Officers Hillsborough currently has one police dog: Jett. Jett is a dual-purpose patrol dog in that she can perform patrol search duties in addition to protecting her partner and apprehending suspect. Jett joined the department in 2018. The German shepherd dog was named by students in Hillsborough. Police dogs typically retire between 6 and 10 years of age. They live with their handlers.', 'Canine Officers Hillsborough currently has one police dog: Jett. Jett is a dual-purpose patrol dog in that she can perform patrol search duties in addition to protecting her partner and apprehending suspect. Jett joined the department in 2018. The German shepherd dog was named by students in Hillsborough. Police dogs typically retire between 6 and 10 years of age. They live with their handlers. Demonstrations The canine officer and handler demonstrate their work for local schools and community groups. To request a demonstration, submit a Police Department contact form or call the police sergeant or senior administrative support specialist. Training The police dogs are certified annually by an independent trainer. Each month, the canine officers and their handlers train for at least 16 hours in a variety of scenarios and in different locations and weather conditions. They also train with handlers and dogs from surrounding agencies.']

Name: 57_burial_cemetery_section_zone
Representative_Docs: ["Grave openings and burials may take place in the Town Cemetery only by a licensed funeral director under the town's supervision. The town must be notified at least one work day in advance by funeral homes or monument companies for grave marking. The town does not mark graves on weekends. Graves will be opened at least 5 feet from the bottom except for cremations. All graves must be level with surrounding areas. Each burial plot is limited to the interment of one of the following: One human body. One human body and one cremation urn. Up to four cremation urns. Purchase and Speculation of Burial Rights All cemetery plots have been sold. Burial rights may not be sold or exchanged for a profit or gain, and burial rights may not be acquired at an in-town rate for the burial of a nonresident. The fee for each burial plot was $500 for a town resident and $1,000 for a nonresident. Certificate of Burial Right A Certificate of Burial Right was issued to the person who purchased a burial right. It identifies the purchaser and specific lots and plots purchased. It entitles the owner to use the designated spaces as a place of burial for any person, subject to the terms and conditions of the Hillsborough Cemetery Ordinance. Upon the owner's death, all rights of the certificate pass to the owner's heirs, legatees or devisees in the same manner as other interests in personal property.", "Location: 200 E. Name: 60_greenway_collins_ridge_ridgewalk
Representative_Docs: ['This project was first introduced when Collins Ridge was seeking  approval for its Master Plan. Conditions of the approval require the developers to reserve and make available to the town land to accommodate pedestrian and bicycle connectivity and to work with the town to determine the location and specific design details for pedestrian and bicycle connectivity between the parcel south of I-85 and the public rights-of-way, sidewalks, greenways, and trails in Collins Ridge. This project was identified by the Board of Commissioners as a transportation priority in September 2021. Outside Groups Coordination with the North Carolina Department of Transportation will be required as the greenway must cross Interstate 85. The current plan is for a greenway and wildlife path underneath a bridge near Collins Ridge. The town must also work with the North Carolina Railroad Company to cross the rail corridor.', 'The proposed Ridgewalk greenway would be a paved, accessible urban greenway for pedestrians and bicyclists that connects downtown Hillsborough to the future train station site and Cates Creek Park in Waterstone. Background This project was first introduced when Collins Ridge was seeking  approval for its Master Plan. Conditions of the approval require the developers to reserve and make available to the town land to accommodate pedestrian and bicycle connectivity and to work with the town to determine the location and specific design details for pedestrian and bicycle connectivity between the parcel south of I-85 and the public rights-of-way, sidewalks, greenways, and trails in Collins Ridge. This project was identified by the Board of Commissioners as a transportation priority in September 2021. Outside Groups Coordination with the North Carolina Department of Transportation will be required as the greenway must cross Interstate 85. The current plan is for a greenway and wildlife path underneath a bridge near Collins Ridge. The town must also work with the North Carolina Railroad Company to cross the rail corridor. Location and Sections It is likely that the greenway project will be implemented in sections. The main section will be between downtown and the train station. The train station could be complete in Fiscal Year 2028, and the town plans to complete the Ridgewalk greenway connection around the same time. The second section will connect the train station to the section of greenway being built and dedicated to the town by the developers of Collins Ridge. The third section will connect Collins Ridge to Cates Creek Park. Current Phase Preliminary engineering and discussion with North Carolina Department of Transportation and the North Carolina Railroad Company have begun. More information will be provided as engineering on projects that must be finished first are completed. Documents Documents Ridgewalk Greenway Feasibility Study Phase 1 (2023) Ridgewalk Greenway Feasibility Study Phase 2 (2024) Ridgewalk Greenway Benefits Analysis Contact Information Town Hall, 101 E. Name: 61_fiber_agreement_google_internet
Representative_Docs: ['Internet Service Providers Brightspeed Brightspeed has a Certificate of Public Convenience from the North Carolina Public Utilities Commission. This agreement allows the town 30 days to review plans, which must be approved if state conditions are met. Google Fiber The town has a master encroachment agreement with Google Fiber. Lumos The town has a master encroachment agreement with Lumos. Lumos is under contract with Orange County to provide broadband internet to rural areas. Spectrum Spectrum\xa0has expressed interest in bringing its fiber internet service to Hillsborough. The company has a franchise agreement with the state to provide video programming services. This agreement will allow the town 30 days to review plans, which must be approved if state conditions are met.', 'Internet Service Providers Brightspeed Brightspeed has a Certificate of Public Convenience from the North Carolina Public Utilities Commission. This agreement allows the town 30 days to review plans, which must be approved if state conditions are met. Google Fiber The town has a master encroachment agreement with Google Fiber. Lumos The town has a master encroachment agreement with Lumos. Lumos is under contract with Orange County to provide broadband internet to rural areas. Spectrum Spectrum\xa0has expressed interest in bringing its fiber internet service to Hillsborough. The company has a franchise agreement with the state to provide video programming services. This agreement will allow the town 30 days to review plans, which must be approved if state conditions are met. Work within Public Rights of Way The utility companies have the legal right to operate within rights of way of the town and of the North Carolina Department of Transportation. The town can impose additional conditions in its rights of way through encroachment agreements with utilities that do not already have special agreements with the state. For utilities that have special agreements with the state, the town only may require utilities to keep roads functional, follow existing laws and repair any damage caused in the rights of way. Work on Private Property Any activity on privately owned property requires approval of the property owner. Utility companies may have legal right to operate in some private areas through a utility easement managed by a homeowners association or other private owner. The town does not have authority to regulate work on private property. This includes final connections between the street and house.', "Private utility companies are installing gigabit fiber-optic cables throughout Hillsborough. The installations should bring more services, capabilities and choices for those living and doing business in our community. Fiber-optic cable installation near Churton Street Project Ownership The Town of Hillsborough is not performing or paying for this work. The installations are private projects owned and operated by the utility companies and their contractors. The state regulates excavation on public and private property to prevent damage to underground utilities. See more information on the North Carolina Law page of the North Carolina 811 website. Internet Service Providers Brightspeed Brightspeed has a Certificate of Public Convenience from the North Carolina Public Utilities Commission. This agreement allows the town 30 days to review plans, which must be approved if state conditions are met. Google Fiber The town has a master encroachment agreement with Google Fiber. Lumos The town has a master encroachment agreement with Lumos. Lumos is under contract with Orange County to provide broadband internet to rural areas. Spectrum Spectrum\xa0has expressed interest in bringing its fiber internet service to Hillsborough. The company has a franchise agreement with the state to provide video programming services. This agreement will allow the town 30 days to review plans, which must be approved if state conditions are met. Work within Public Rights of Way The utility companies have the legal right to operate within rights of way of the town and of the North Carolina Department of Transportation. The town can impose additional conditions in its rights of way through encroachment agreements with utilities that do not already have special agreements with the state. For utilities that have special agreements with the state, the town only may require utilities to keep roads functional, follow existing laws and repair any damage caused in the rights of way. Work on Private Property Any activity on privately owned property requires approval of the property owner. Utility companies may have legal right to operate in some private areas through a utility easement managed by a homeowners association or other private owner. The town does not have authority to regulate work on private property. This includes final connections between the street and house. What to Expect All Fiber Companies Subcontractors Subcontractors are performing the fiber installations for all internet service providers. The subcontractors should have proper identification with them at all times while performing work. Their vehicles should be marked with a legible magnetic decal or similar marking that identifies the vehicles as a subcontractor for the companies and provides a contact phone number. Markings Excavation requires notification to North Carolina 811 to allow utilities to mark underground lines for safety. The work may proceed if utilities have not been marked within the prescribed time period and if no visible indications of underground utilities exist at the site. Several weeks before construction, paint markings will be sprayed on the ground. The paint typically disappears in a few weeks. For more information regarding markings, see the North Carolina 811 website. Color Codes The uniform color codes mandated by the American Public Works Association for temporarily marking utility lines are: Red — Electric power lines, cables, conduit and lighting cables Orange — Telecommunication, alarm or signal lines, cables or conduit Yellow — Natural gas, oil, steam, petroleum or other flammable Green — Sewers and drain lines Blue — Potable drinking water Purple — Reclaimed water, irrigation and slurry lines Pink — Temporary survey markings, unknown/unidentified facilities White — Proposed excavation limits or routes Construction Construction Type and Additions Trenching and horizontal boring are being used to excavate in rights of way. The trenching involves digging a shallower and narrower trench than traditional excavation methods. It can help in avoiding tree roots and existing underground utilities. Horizontal boring machines are being used for tight rights of way, deeper installation needs or the crossing of driveways. Periodic utility vaults are being installed in the right of way. Smaller plastic fiber vaults are being installed on property lines for individual service, similar in location to phone and cable provider boxes. The vaults are like water meter boxes in appearance. They are typically gray and are being placed at the same grade as the right of way. Construction Area This image is from Orange County's Geographic Information System website with the photography layer turned on. Town staff added the black and blue to highlight the street and right of way. A public right of way is designated between most streets and the adjacent private property. This is where service providers are allowed by law to place their fiber along with water, sewer and electrical lines. The right of way typically extends to the water meter box and sewer cleanout pipe on a property. Residents can review the approximate location of the rights of way for most streets on Orange County’s Geographic Information System website. Enter an address and select the photography button in the upper right corner to see property lines and streets. Contractors should receive permission from property owners before working on private property if permission is not already granted through an easement. This would include townhouse, condominium and apartment complexes. Construction Hours Work within town rights of way will typically occur from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. weekdays. Permission to expand this schedule will be given sometimes on a case-by-case basis. Post-Construction In most cases and weather permitting, private property and public rights of way should be restored to original condition within three to five business days once construction in an area is complete. Companies are not required by law to coordinate their activities. Once the work of one company is complete, the construction process could start again by another company. Construction in your area does not mean that new or improved service will be available to you immediately. Residents should reach out to the internet service providers directly for availability timelines. Google Fiber In addition to the expectations for all internet service providers, the Google Fiber subcontractor should distribute informational door hangers in advance of construction in your neighborhood. Questions and Complaints Register formal complaints with the state by contacting the North Carolina Attorney General’s Office . Construction Concerns or Reporting Damages For questions or concerns about construction in your area, communicate with the crew working in the area. If additional discussion is needed, call the number on the subcontractor vehicle or door hanger or use the contact information below for assistance. If you are concerned for contractor safety, contact the Occupational Safety and Health Administration directly. Call 911 immediately if you see a danger threatening life or health. Brightspeed Brightspeed Customer Service Phone Line — 833-692-7773 Google Fiber Google Support/Construction Phone Line ― 877-454-6959, Option 3 Google Support Website ― fiber.google.com/support Lumos Director of Market Development Andrew Stevenson: Phone ― 336-821-8801 Email ― andrew.stevenson@lumosfiber.com Service Questions Use these contacts if you have questions about new service or want to report problems with existing service: AT&T ― 866-861-6075 Brightspeed ― 833-692-7773 Lumos ― 855-465-8667 Google ― 866-777-7550 Spectrum ― 855-366-7132"]

Name: 62_disabilities_animals_americans_modifications
Representative_Docs: ['The Town of Hillsborough does not discriminate on the basis of disability in its services, programs or activities. See the Americans with Disabilities Act Policy page\xa0for more information.', 'Modifications to Services Effective Communication The town will make reasonable modifications to policies and procedures to ensure that people with disabilities have an equal opportunity to enjoy programs, services and activities. Requests should be made as soon as possible, preferably 15 days before the activity or event. Example modifications are below. Effective Communication —\xa0The town will provide auxiliary aids and services upon request to provide effective communication for people with disabilities, including qualified sign language interpreters, assistive listening devices, documents in Braille, and other methods of making communications accessible to people who have speech, hearing, or vision impairments. Service Animals —\xa0People with service animals are welcome in Town of Hillsborough offices, even where pets and other animals are prohibited. Special Solid Waste Collection — The town provides special garbage collection for residents with disabilities who live alone or have no able-bodied person in the household. See the Garbage Collection page.', 'Effective Communication The town will make reasonable modifications to policies and procedures to ensure that people with disabilities have an equal opportunity to enjoy programs, services and activities. Name: 63_86_fleet_facility_operations
Representative_Docs: ['Location and Background Location The Highway 86 North Facility is located at 890 N.C. 86 N. in Hillsborough. The building currently serves as office, storage and work space for Fleet Maintenance Operations and the Safety and Risk Management Office. The renovation and expansion will allow the Public Works Division to move its operations out of a floodplain. The division currently operates out of an undersized building and mobile offices in a gravel lot at 719 Dimmocks Mill Road\xa0behind the Adron F. Thompson Water/Sewer Facility. Funding Funding The estimated cost of the improvements is nearly $3.7 million. The town expects to pay for about $1 million of the project in cash and to finance the rest. Name: 66_sewers_lawndale_sewer_project
Representative_Docs: ['About the Project Orange County installed the sewers in this area under a grant program in the 1980s before the town assumed ownership. The sewers now need repair or replacement. A hired contractor studied the condition of the sewers and the sewer service connections from homes in the Lawndale Basin. The study included monitoring flow in the sewers during different times of day and storm events. Part of the project will involve installing a sewer cleanout pipe where one may be missing.\xa0The pipe allows access to the sewer line to remove blockages. It helps specify between public and private ownership. The town maintains the public portion of sewer lines. Property owners are responsible for the section of sewer service lines that connects to their structure from the right of way or easement.', "About the Project Orange County installed the sewers in this area under a grant program in the 1980s before the town assumed ownership. The sewers now need repair or replacement. A hired contractor studied the condition of the sewers and the sewer service connections from homes in the Lawndale Basin. The study included monitoring flow in the sewers during different times of day and storm events. Part of the project will involve installing a sewer cleanout pipe where one may be missing.\xa0The pipe allows access to the sewer line to remove blockages. It helps specify between public and private ownership. The town maintains the public portion of sewer lines. Property owners are responsible for the section of sewer service lines that connects to their structure from the right of way or easement. Lining a Sewer Roots and soil can enter a pipe and clog it. Cracks in the pipe also can let sewage out. Water that enters sewer pipes must be treated by the town. The blue coloring shows a relined pipe. When a sewer is relined, the service connection from a home or building is blocked temporarily. The relining process reduces disturbance to roads and yards because the work is performed by machines underground. To line a sewer: A flexible resin inverted “sock” is inserted into the sewer main through manholes. The sock is cured or hardened to the pipe's shape. Crews use hot steam or water or sometimes ultraviolet light for the curing process. A machine cuts openings in the relined pipe for any sewer services connected to the sewer main. Project Contact Julie Taylor Taylor Engineering & Consulting 336-339-6883 Contact Taylor Engineering", "Improvements are planned to the sewer system within the Lawndale Basin in northern Hillsborough. The project will involve: Cleaning and inspecting sewers. Performing small dig and repair projects on the main sewer line and on select sewer services\xa0within the right of way. Moving or installing some sewer cleanouts. Lining sewer pipes to prevent groundwater and rainwater from entering the sewer system. The town maintains a part of the sewer service line — from within the road right of way to the sewer main. A sewer cleanout is typically on the line between the property's edge and the public right of way. Site Project area The Lawndale Basin is in northern Hillsborough. The project area includes: An easement between Rainey Avenue and Torain Street. Parts of Lawndale and Rainey avenues and parts of Cornelius, Hill and Torain streets. Funding Funding The project is estimated to cost about $1.5 million. The town will receive a low-interest loan of about $1.1 million from the Clean Water State Revolving Fund, which includes loan forgiveness for up to $500,000 of the funding. The remaining part of the project will be paid for from reserve funds and should have no impact on future customer rates. Current Phase The rehabilitation and replacement project is expected to begin in November 2025. Project Details About the Project Orange County installed the sewers in this area under a grant program in the 1980s before the town assumed ownership. The sewers now need repair or replacement. A hired contractor studied the condition of the sewers and the sewer service connections from homes in the Lawndale Basin. The study included monitoring flow in the sewers during different times of day and storm events. Part of the project will involve installing a sewer cleanout pipe where one may be missing.\xa0The pipe allows access to the sewer line to remove blockages. It helps specify between public and private ownership. The town maintains the public portion of sewer lines. Property owners are responsible for the section of sewer service lines that connects to their structure from the right of way or easement. Lining a Sewer Roots and soil can enter a pipe and clog it. Cracks in the pipe also can let sewage out. Water that enters sewer pipes must be treated by the town. The blue coloring shows a relined pipe. When a sewer is relined, the service connection from a home or building is blocked temporarily. The relining process reduces disturbance to roads and yards because the work is performed by machines underground. To line a sewer: A flexible resin inverted “sock” is inserted into the sewer main through manholes. The sock is cured or hardened to the pipe's shape. Crews use hot steam or water or sometimes ultraviolet light for the curing process. A machine cuts openings in the relined pipe for any sewer services connected to the sewer main. Project Contact Julie Taylor Taylor Engineering & Consulting 336-339-6883 Contact Taylor Engineering What to Expect Sewer Service Issues When workers reline the sewer pipes: The sewer service connection from your home to the sewer line will be blocked. Pressure in the sewer line can force water out of toilets and drains. The contractor will communicate directly with residents when service disruption will occur. Service connections will be reopened at the end of the noted workday. Avoid a Sewer Backup On the day your service is disrupted: Do not do laundry, dishwashing, bathing or other activities that use large amounts of water. Keep toilets flushed after use. Place a towel on the rim of your toilet bowl and keep the seat and lid closed. Cover any basement floor drains. Odor The curing process for lining a sewer may produce an odor, like glue or a permanent marker. The odor may enter your home through the pipe that connects your home to the public sewer main. The odor is caused by styrene, a naturally occurring chemical, used in the resin of the liners. The airborne concentration levels are minimal and do not pose a health risk. During short-term exposure, the chemical can cause eye irritation, headache and discomfort. The odor will disappear once the pipe lining is fully hardened. Avoid Potential Odor To Avoid the Odor — Run water into sinks, bathtubs and drains to fill the P-trap fitting on pipes, creating a water barrier. About a gallon of water will fill the U-shaped portion of the pipe, which keeps sewer gas and other vapors out of your home or building. If Odor Is Inside — Open windows or use fans to refresh and circulate your home’s air. If Odor Remains — If the odor remains despite the steps above, contact a plumber about fixing a potential open air connection to the sewer. That problem is a health risk from sewer gas entering your home. Additional Items Timeline and Hours — The project is expected to start in November 2025. Once the project moves forward, it is expected to take about six months to complete. Construction activities will be 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Monday-Friday unless approved in writing by the town. Construction — Noise and traffic disruptions will occur throughout the project. Contractors may need to dig in the road or rights of way to make repairs. They will use manholes or sewer cleanouts in the right of way to reline sewers. One lane of traffic may be closed. If repairs are needed on private property, the contractor will coordinate with the resident. Markings — Workers will place paint markings or flags on the ground to identify underground utilities where digging may occur. Paint markings wash off with time. Please do not disrupt the project markings. Equipment — If equipment or materials are stored on private property, the contractor should have a written contract with the property owner. Post-construction — The contractor will restore areas disturbed by the project. Contact Information Town Hall Annex, 105 E. Corbin St. 919-296-9631 Please make an appointment for in-person visits. Contact Utilities Staff Title Phone Email Strandwitz, Marie Utilities Director 919-296-9631 Email"]

Name: 70_training_safely_officers_jiu
Representative_Docs: ["Internal Training The Police Department offers a variety of training in house, including the following. Jiu Jitsu Training The Police Department provides training in Brazilian jiu jitsu to increase officers' confidence in their ability to safely bring calm to intense and dangerous situations. The intent  is to reduce the need for use of force. Jiu jitsu is a martial arts grappling technique that emphasizes taking an opponent to the ground, gaining a dominant position and using techniques to safely force the person into submission. The techniques allow dominance from any position and negate size and strength advantages. They allow officers to safely take someone into custody, including: Safely handcuffing a person. Safely stopping a person from hitting or hurting an officer. Safely extracting a person's arms if the person is face down possibly with a firearm or a weapon underneath. Virtual Training The Police Department owns a virtual reality training system that allows up to two officers to train together in a small space with a controlled environment.\xa0The training helps officers: Fine-tune work done on a daily basis, like vehicle stops. Learn how to safely respond to a variety of situations. Review where improvements are needed in their response. Officers put on a headset and trackers for their wrist and tools. They train in scenarios created by the department or provided through the system, such as an active shooter situation. Officers are told what their scenario will be and the expected outcome. Their responses are unscripted. A trainer monitors the training on a screen, where avatars for the training officers can be seen.\xa0A body camera view allows the trainer to see where the officer’s eyes are. The virtual training can: Be manipulated to interact with the officers and their responses. Provide training for more real-life situations than basic training can offer. External Training Hillsborough officers have achieved certification in a number of trainings offered externally. Crisis Intervention Crisis intervention training creates connections among law enforcement, mental health providers, hospital emergency services and individuals with mental illness and their families. The training improves communication, identifies mental health resources for those in crisis, and ensures officer and community safety. Law Enforcement Executive Program The Law Enforcement Executive Program at N.C. State University provides twenty days of training over six months to effectively manage\xa0critical challenges. Participants enter the program with\xa0good basic managerial skills and a strong working knowledge of North Carolina local government and police service. Through the training, they build on those foundations and: Explore applying public management ideas and techniques for police agencies. Sharpen personal skills to excel as police managers. Think creatively about law enforcement management in increasingly complex, uncertain and changing environments. Professional Certification Officers can earn intermediate and advanced law enforcement certificates through the\xa0Law Enforcement Officers’ Professional Certificate Program. The program was established by the Criminal Justice Education and Training Standards Commission to: Foster interest in college education and professional law enforcement training Attract highly qualified people into law enforcement West Point Leadership Training The West Point Leadership Course at Methodist University in Fayetteville is adapted from the military leadership course that is required of all cadets in their third year of study at the United States Military Academy at West Point. The academically rigorous course is adapted for use in non-military organizations and relies heavily on case-based learning. Name: 72_page_county_greenways_hillsborough
Representative_Docs: ['Parks and Greenways Hillsborough has green spaces nestled within its trees, including the Riverwalk greenway, which follows the Eno River through downtown. See the town’s parks and greenways on the Recreation Facilities page. View recreation facilities available across the county on the Orange County website.', "New to Town? See information on this page\xa0to help you quickly get settled and feeling at home in our small town. Want to Get More Involved? See information on this page to help you learn more about the town. Volunteering for pollinator garden workdays is a great way to meet other Hillsborough residents. Stormwater Fees Tax Rates Water and Sewer Rates Economic Development Around Town Arts and Events Hillsborough is home to a thriving arts and culture scene, as well as many events. View galleries, shops and upcoming events on the Hillsborough Visitors Center website. Development Private Development Private development is a project not undertaken by the town or any other government. The town does list large private development occurring or under review within town limits on the Development Projects page. Public Projects See what the town is developing on the Public Projects page. History Hillsborough is home to a thriving arts and culture scene, as well as many events. View galleries, shops and upcoming events on the Hillsborough Visitors Center website. Parks and Greenways Hillsborough has green spaces nestled within its trees, including the Riverwalk greenway, which follows the Eno River through downtown. See the town’s parks and greenways on the Recreation Facilities page. View recreation facilities available across the county on the Orange County website. Be Informed Emergency Alerts The town issues emergency notifications through OC Alerts , Orange County's mass notification system. The system is a partnership of Orange Water and Sewer Authority and the governments in Orange County. OWASA provides water service to the Carrboro and Chapel Hill community. See the Ready Hillsborough page for emergency resources. Town News The town provides regular updates through news releases, social media and a monthly newsletter . A weekly digest of town news releases and key reminders are emailed on Fridays. Time-sensitive emails are sent as needed. The newsletter is inserted into paper water and sewer bills and included in the weekly digest at the start of each month. Follow @HillsboroughGov on Facebook , Instagram and X . Follow Town of Hillsborough on Nextdoor . Subscribe to town emails. Get Involved Board Meetings Meetings of the elected and appointed boards are open to the public. They typically are in the Town Hall Annex, 105 E. Corbin St. Hillsborough Board of Commissioners — Meets the second and fourth Monday of each month, typically taking a break in July. Appointed Boards — See their individual\xa0pages for meeting schedules. Subscribe to meeting notices and see the agenda and minutes site for meeting information and materials. Civic Engagement Course The town offers a course over several weeks aimed at helping community members increase their knowledge of town government, as well as their interest and ability in influencing and participating in town decisions. See the Government 101 page. Engage Hillsborough The town offers periodic events for community members and staff to connect and discuss a topic. The town also seeks input at times through surveys. Check the Engage Hillsborough page for current or upcoming ways to engage. Volunteer Opportunities The town offers multiple ways to volunteer or help others in the community. Consider: Serving on appointed boards . Serving as a community liaison to help share information from the town and let staff know of concerns and questions in the community. Contact Communications . Improving the town’s public spaces\xa0by joining a team to remove invasive plants or attending various workdays, including to remove trash from roadsides and help with pollinator gardens. Contact Public Space . Donating to the Water Assistance Program to help neighbors pay their water bills. See a Quick Guide to Services Public Safety Emergency workers are dispatched through 911. Fire — Orange Rural Fire Department provides fire service to Hillsborough through a contract. Law Enforcement — Hillsborough Police Department provides police services within town limits. Orange County Sheriff’s Office provides services in unincorporated Orange County. Property improvements or uses Contact the Town Contact the Hillsborough Planning and Economic Development Division using the contact form or by calling 919-732-1270 before signing contracts or purchasing supplies for: Adding a deck or room or enclosing an open space. Making yard improvements such as outbuildings, fences and patios. Adding accessory dwelling units. Running a business from your home. Contact the County Call the Orange County Building Inspections Division at 919-245-2575 before signing contracts or purchasing supplies for: Renovating your home. Changing or improving electrical, heating, air conditioning or plumbing systems in your home. Adding any systems to an outbuilding. Street Maintenance The Hillsborough Public Works Division oversees maintenance of town-owned streets. Some roads in town are private or maintained by the state. See the Street Maintenance page to learn more. Solid Waste Bulk Items The town collects bulk items, such as large appliances, monthly on the second full week of the month, with the week starting on Sunday. See the Garbage Collection page for items that can be collected. Garbage The Public Works\xa0Division provides residential curbside garbage collection services within town limits as a service paid for by property taxes.\xa0To start residential service, call 919-296-9600 or use the Contact Public Works form. Multi-family dwellings receive commercial services through independent vendors. For routes and services, see the Curbside Collections Calendar . For information on special collections and requirements, see the Garbage Collection page. Rollout Carts Video Recycling Orange County provides recycling collection services on Wednesdays in Hillsborough.\xa0To set up new recycling service: Use the Orange County recycling contact form . Email recycling@orangecountync.gov . Call 919-968-2788. Rollout Carts Video Yard Waste Yard waste is collected weekly. Leaves can be placed in compostable bags year-round with a limit of 10 bags per household each week. Loose leaves are collected\xa0November through January. During the seasonal loose leaves collection, the town offers delivery of bulk leaves for compost or mulch. See the Yard Waste Collection page for requirements and additional information. Water and Sewer Accounts and Billing Hillsborough provides water and sewer services within town limits and to some properties outside town limits. Connection —\xa0Service should be requested at least three business days in advance. See the Start or Stop Service page. Electronic Bills and Online Payment —\xa0Water and sewer customers are encouraged to sign up for electronic bills and to pay online through the payments and donations site . Leaks —\xa0Customers may request bill adjustments if a water line break has resulted in extraordinary charges for a given billing period.\xa0Customers are allowed one leak adjustment in a12-month period. See the Water and Sewer Billing and Adjustments page. Rates —\xa0To view and understand the town's water and sewer rates, see the Water and Sewer Rates page. Water and Sewer Services Issues Emergencies —\xa0Report water and sewer emergencies immediately to the Hillsborough Water Treatment Plant at 919-732-3621. The plant is staffed 24 hours. Leave a message. Sewer Cleanout Pipes and Rings — Report broken sewer cleanout pipes and rings to utilities staff. The broken pipe should be repaired quickly to prevent stormwater from entering the sewer system. The pipe's protective ring must be kept in place. The capped pipes are located on the edge of rights of way and provide easy access to a property's main sewer line. Contact Utilities or call 919-296-9651. Water Tampering and Theft —\xa0If you see a person or business taking water from a water hydrant without an orange meter box, report it immediately to 911 and to the Hillsborough Water Distribution and Wastewater Collection Division at 919-296-9651. See the Tampering and Theft page. Water and Sewer Responsibilities Avoid Blocked Pipes Fats, Oils and Grease — Keep fats, oils and grease out of drains. They will stick to plumbing and sewer lines and eventually cause sewer backups or overflows. Prevent this by: Discarding used fats, oils and grease with other garbage. Minimizing use of garbage disposals. Scraping plates into trash cans. Using paper towels to wipe leftover oils and fats from pots and pans. The town provides fat trappers to water and sewer customers at the Town Hall Annex, 105 E. Corbin St. Contact Utilities or call 919-296-9653. Wipes — Flush the 3 P's only (pee, poo and toilet paper). Anything else can lead to overflows. Wipes labeled as flushable are not flushable and will clog the sewer system. No Wipes in the Pipes Video Be Aware of Easements and Town Assets Easements —\xa0Water and sewer easements give the Utilities Department the right to access, maintain, repair or replace a town asset, such as a water or sewer main. The town will not replace any plants, fences, sheds, fountains, patios, decks or other structures that impede access to the easement and maintenance of the infrastructure. Fences over easements require an encroachment agreement and other permissions. Contact Utilities or call 919-296-9630 before installing a fence. Private Service Lines —\xa0Homeowners are responsible for these private service lines: The water line between the meter and the home The sewer line between the cleanout pipe and the home. The cleanout pipe's protective ring must be kept in place. The capped pipe provides easy access to your home's main sewer line. Utility Lines and Devices —\xa0Know where utility lines are before digging, and leave access around fire hydrants and other utility devices. Call 811 to have public lines marked for free. Private service lines require marking from private companies. See the North Carolina 811 website. Follow Irrigation Rules Backflow Preventer — Customers with pools or separate irrigation systems must have a backflow preventer. See Backflow Prevention . Spray Irrigation — Spray irrigation requires a separate meter and has limited days and hours for use. Systems must have moisture sensors and should be checked annually for leaks. See: Irrigation Systems on the Water Conservation page Start or Stop Service page", 'Arts and Events Hillsborough is home to a thriving arts and culture scene, as well as many events. View galleries, shops and upcoming events on the Hillsborough Visitors Center website. Development Private Development Private development is a project not undertaken by the town or any other government. The town does list large private development occurring or under review within town limits on the Development Projects page. Public Projects See what the town is developing on the Public Projects page. History Hillsborough is home to a thriving arts and culture scene, as well as many events. View galleries, shops and upcoming events on the Hillsborough Visitors Center website. Parks and Greenways Hillsborough has green spaces nestled within its trees, including the Riverwalk greenway, which follows the Eno River through downtown. See the town’s parks and greenways on the Recreation Facilities page. View recreation facilities available across the county on the Orange County website.']

Name: 73_banners_banner_feet_flagpoles
Representative_Docs: ['Requirements for Banners The only approved location for banners is between the flagpoles that are part of the “Welcome to Hillsborough” sign at the intersection of South Churton and Mayo streets. Banners may be displayed for two weeks. They may advertise community activities, functions, festivals and similar events open to the public. Functions of a political or for-profit nature are not permitted. Banners must be: Made of a mesh material or have air vents every 3 feet. Tied to the banner supports using one-quarter-inch nylon rope at each corner. Flagpoles cannot be used. About 30 inches tall, with a maximum height of 36 inches and a maximum length of 15 feet. The banner supports are spaced about 18 feet apart.', 'General Information Reservation and permit applications must be submitted at least three business days before signage is placed. Town staff will remove any banners or event signs improperly located, exceeding size limits or left beyond the permitted time. Requirements for Banners The only approved location for banners is between the flagpoles that are part of the “Welcome to Hillsborough” sign at the intersection of South Churton and Mayo streets. Banners may be displayed for two weeks. They may advertise community activities, functions, festivals and similar events open to the public. Functions of a political or for-profit nature are not permitted. Banners must be: Made of a mesh material or have air vents every 3 feet. Tied to the banner supports using one-quarter-inch nylon rope at each corner. Flagpoles cannot be used. About 30 inches tall, with a maximum height of 36 inches and a maximum length of 15 feet. The banner supports are spaced about 18 feet apart. Requirements for Additional Signs No event may display more than five additional signs. Total signage is limited to 100 square feet. On Site\xa0— One sign no larger than 32 square feet may be on the lot hosting the event for the same display period. The sign may be a banner if it is securely fastened and adequately vented to pose no traffic threat. Off Site\xa0— Four signs may be displayed off site in locations noted in the permit application. They may be placed in the public right of way but not in a driveway or intersection sight triangle. The signs must be rigid (not banners), and a single side may not exceed 8 square feet. Signs may be erected 14 days before the event and must be removed within 48 hours after the event. This allows for a maximum display of 17 days.', 'Location — Intersection of South Churton and Mayo streets The town’s location for special event banners is reservable for up to two weeks for public events in Hillsborough or its planning jurisdiction. The banner location is part of the “Welcome to Hillsborough” sign. Fees The fees for the special event banner are: Banner-only reservation and permit — $5. Banner reservation and up to five additional signs permit — $20. Fees and Charges Schedule Requirements Special event banners and signage require a zoning compliance permit from the town. In addition to the reservable banner location, up\xa0to five signs may be placed around town with the permit. Town staff will remove any banners or event signs improperly located, exceeding size limits or left beyond the permitted time. Requirements for Banners The only approved location for banners is between the flagpoles that are part of the “Welcome to Hillsborough” sign at the intersection of South Churton and Mayo streets. Banners may be displayed for two weeks. They may advertise community activities, functions, festivals and similar events open to the public. Functions of a political or for-profit nature are not permitted. Banners must be: Made of a mesh material or have air vents every 3 feet. Tied to the banner supports using one-quarter-inch nylon rope at each corner. Flagpoles cannot be used. About 30 inches tall, with a maximum height of 36 inches and a maximum length of 15 feet. The banner supports are spaced about 18 feet apart. Requirements for Additional Signs No event may display more than five additional signs. Total signage is limited to 100 square feet. On Site\xa0— One sign no larger than 32 square feet may be on the lot hosting the event for the same display period. The sign may be a banner if it is securely fastened and adequately vented to pose no traffic threat. Off Site\xa0— Four signs may be displayed off site in locations noted in the permit application. They may be placed in the public right of way but not in a driveway or intersection sight triangle. The signs must be rigid (not banners), and a single side may not exceed 8 square feet. Signs may be erected 14 days before the event and must be removed within 48 hours after the event. This allows for a maximum display of 17 days. Reserve the Banner See Available Times Reserve Special Event Banner Contact Information Town Hall, 101 E. Orange St. 919-296-9470 Office Hours — 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. Monday-Friday Please make an appointment for in-person visits. Contact Reservations Administrator']

Name: 74_parking_downtown_strategies_demand
Representative_Docs: ['Parking Study —\xa0The town is comprehensively studying its parking supply and the demand for parking in the downtown area and West Hillsborough to address existing conditions and future needs. See the Parking Study page.', 'Effective parking strategies are essential as the town continues to invest in revitalizing its downtown area, including West Hillsborough.\xa0A strategic parking management plan will allow Hillsborough to maximize the potential of its downtown for commercial, residential and tourism growth. Goals for this project are to: Assess the current supply and demand of parking in downtown Hillsborough. Understand perceptions about parking in the area. Analyze parking needs to proactively address challenges in the downtown parking infrastructure. Identify opportunities to improve the accessibility, visibility and use of parking in the area. Develop strategies to manage existing parking resources with broader community needs and the evolving demands of downtown users. Align parking strategies with previous planning initiatives, including the Comprehensive Sustainability Plan. Create a long-lasting parking management plan that supports existing downtown improvement efforts.', 'Goals Effective parking strategies are essential as the town continues to invest in revitalizing its downtown area, including West Hillsborough.\xa0A strategic parking management plan will allow Hillsborough to maximize the potential of its downtown for commercial, residential and tourism growth. Goals for this project are to: Assess the current supply and demand of parking in downtown Hillsborough. Understand perceptions about parking in the area. Analyze parking needs to proactively address challenges in the downtown parking infrastructure. Identify opportunities to improve the accessibility, visibility and use of parking in the area. Develop strategies to manage existing parking resources with broader community needs and the evolving demands of downtown users. Align parking strategies with previous planning initiatives, including the Comprehensive Sustainability Plan. Create a long-lasting parking management plan that supports existing downtown improvement efforts.']

Name: 75_enterprise_fund_funds_revenue
Representative_Docs: ['The Stormwater Utility Fund is one of two enterprise funds operated by the town. The other is the Water and Sewer Fund. Enterprise funds account for the business-like activities of the government. These services are funded with revenues generated from fees and charges rather than taxes. No profits are made in enterprise funds, and the revenue is used for no other purpose. The Hillsborough Stormwater Fund pays for stormwater services. The revenue for the fund comes\xa0primarily from stormwater fees. See the Stormwater Management Utility Ordinance .', 'Understanding Enterprise Funds The Water and Sewer Fund is one of two enterprise funds operated by the town. The other is the Stormwater Utility Fund. Enterprise funds account for the business-like activities of the government. These services are funded with revenues generated from fees and charges rather than taxes.\xa0No profits are made in enterprise funds, and the revenue is used for no other purpose. The Hillsborough Water and Sewer Fund pays for providing water and sewer service. The revenue for the fund comes\xa0primarily from water and sewer charges.', 'Understanding Enterprise Funds The Stormwater Utility Fund is one of two enterprise funds operated by the town. The other is the Water and Sewer Fund. Enterprise funds account for the business-like activities of the government. These services are funded with revenues generated from fees and charges rather than taxes. No profits are made in enterprise funds, and the revenue is used for no other purpose. The Hillsborough Stormwater Fund pays for stormwater services. The revenue for the fund comes\xa0primarily from stormwater fees. See the Stormwater Management Utility Ordinance .']

Name: 78_fats_grease_oil_fat
Representative_Docs: ['Fats, Oils and Grease Fats, oil and grease poured down the drain will collect and stick to plumbing and sewer lines. Over time, lines can become blocked, causing sewer backups or overflows. Properties that discharge greasy wastewater are required to have interceptors for fats, oil and grease. Residential customers can obtain a free fat trapper from the town. They also should: Discard\xa0used fats, oil\xa0and grease with other garbage or recycle\xa0at an Orange County solid waste convenience center. Avoid\xa0or limit\xa0use of garbage disposals. Scrape\xa0plates into trash cans. Use\xa0paper towels to wipe leftover oils and fats from pots and pans. Fat Trappers The town provides fat trappers to water and sewer customers to help keep grease and oils out of the sewer system. To receive a fat trapper or additional bags, contact the utilities infrastructure protection supervisor or visit the Town Hall Annex, 105 E. Corbin St., where water bills are paid. Video Fats, Oils and Grease Program No Wipes in the Pipes Only waste and toilet paper should be flushed. Household cleaning and personal hygiene products labeled as flushable or safe for sewer or septic systems do not dissolve. Additionally, do not dispose of trash, medicine or other items in the sewer system. These items clog sewer lines and can damage sewer pumps and other equipment. For more information, contact the utilities infrastructure protection supervisor. Name: 79_commissioners_board_representing_mayor
Representative_Docs: ['The board has three members: One connected with businesses that collect the room occupancy tax. Two who are active in promoting travel and tourism in town. One representing the Hillsborough Board of Commissioners and serving on the Tourism Board.', 'One representing the Hillsborough Board of Commissioners and serving on the Tourism Board.', "The Hillsborough Board of Commissioners is the town's legislative body and establishes policy and law. Name: 80_medical_injury_injuries_applied
Representative_Docs: ['Medical Attention After any use of force situation has stabilized, the first and foremost duty of every officer is to assess those involved for injuries and seek any medical intervention needed. Emergency Medical Services shall be contacted any time a subject: Has a visible injury. Is complaining of an injury or medical condition, such as chest pains or difficulty breathing. Has had a conducted energy weapon (Taser) or pepper spray applied and the circumstances described in the Less-Lethal Provisions exist of the person being in control of a vehicle in gear or motion or being in a position that creates the likelihood of significant additional injury other than those effects reasonably anticipated due to the use of the aerosol spray. Has been struck with an impact weapon or object that could reasonably be expected to have caused and injury. Has had any force that could be classified as deadly force applied. Notification of On-Duty Supervisor The on-duty supervisor shall be notified and respond to the scene any time an officer uses force above the level of soft-hand guidance. Unless it is necessary for safety, medical reasons or for purposes of diffusing the situation, the suspect shall not be moved from the initial location until the supervisor has arrived. Secure the Scene The scene of the use of force incident shall be secured and any witnesses identified and separated. Anything of evidentiary value shall be identified and not disturbed initially. Collecting Evidence and Photographs of Injuries All evidence shall be collected and turned in to property. Any audio and video recordings of the event are to be identified and retained for the review. Whenever feasible, photographs are to be taken of any injury or alleged injury, including to document the absence of visible injuries when a complaint of injury is made and no injury is apparent. Officers with visible injuries shall also be photographed. Command Notification All use of force incidents require the on-duty supervisor to make a command notification. For incidents involving a serious injury, this notification must be made immediately by telephone. For all other incidents, notification can be made electronically as soon as practical and should include the report number, employee involved and extent of any injuries. Charging for Resistance and Assault If an officer exercising police authority encounters resistance that clearly justifies a charge of resisting arrest or assaulting an officer, these charges should be made immediately.', 'After any use of force situation has stabilized, the first and foremost duty of every officer is to assess those involved for injuries and seek any medical intervention needed. Name: 81_distribution_maintains_deliver_environment
Representative_Docs: ['The Utilities Department prepares water for drinking and cleans used water for return to the environment. It also maintains pipes and infrastructure to deliver drinking water and to transport wastewater for treatment.', 'Water Distribution Division The Water Distribution Division repairs water line breaks and maintains: 153 miles of water lines 3 water boosting pump stations 5 water tanks The division includes a backflow prevention program to prevent contaminants from entering the water supply. Awards In past years, the division has won\xa0Outstanding Water Distribution Operator of the Year from the N.C. American Water Works Association-Water Environment Association.', 'The Water Distribution Division repairs water line breaks and maintains: 153 miles of water lines 3 water boosting pump stations 5 water tanks The division includes a backflow prevention program to prevent contaminants from entering the water supply. Awards In past years, the division has won\xa0Outstanding Water Distribution Operator of the Year from the N.C. American Water Works Association-Water Environment Association.']

These guidelines meet the current design standards and ensure more uniformity in the design of those projects. Engineers are encouraged to use these standards in their design process. It is not the intent of this manual to provide an explanation for every design problem encountered, nor, as a substitute for experience, sound judgment, and engineering knowledge. If a required standard or detail is not included below, the latest edition of the NCDOT Standard Specifications for Roads and Structures shall apply. Ordinances and Policies The City has other ordinances and policies that relate to new construction projects including the following: Mebane Storm Sewer Design Manual, Mebane Phase 2 Post Construction Ordinance and Manual, Riparian Buffer Protection Ordinance, Water Supply Watershed Ordinance, and the Mebane Unified Development Ordinance. Engineers are to refer to these documents for specific requirements as a part of their design process. The City of Mebane will use these standards and requirements along with sound engineering principles to review the detailed engineering drawings. These standards shall apply to all roadway and utility improvements and developments within the City of Mebane. In addition, these standards apply to areas outside the City Limits that are under the jurisdiction of the City. Final Approvals The City of Mebane has the right of final approval over all construction work done under these guidelines and no work will be accepted until approval is made by the City. Please contact the City Engineer at (919) 563-5901 with any questions regarding these standards and specifications. The standards and details below shall apply to all projects within the City of Mebane and the City's extra-territorial jurisdiction. All plan preparation and construction shall conform to the City of Mebane Standards and Specifications. Updated October, 2021. Name: 0_collection_cart_recycling_waste
Representative_Docs: ['Public Works Frequently Asked Questions How do I find my garbage pickup day? Click here to see the scheduled garbage collection map. Who do I call when my garbage has not been picked up? If you placed your cart at the street prior to 7:00 AM, you may call the Public Works Department at 919-563-3401 to request an out-of-cycle collection. What items can be recycled? The City of Mebane contracts with GFL Environmental to collect and haul acceptable recyclable material for processing. View the GFL website here . I noticed a pothole on a city street. Where do I report it to be repaired? Call 919-563-3401 and provide the name of the street and closest address to the pothole. How do I dispose of electronic waste (e-waste)? The State of North Carolina banned TV’s and computers from being placed in the landfill. The Alamance County Landfill accepts most anything electronic based (or most anything with a plug). The landfill is located at 2701 Austin Quarter Rd. in Graham, NC. How do I dispose of used tires? Used tires are not collected by the city and must be taken to the Alamance County Landfill located at 2701 Austin Quarter Rd. in Graham, NC. How do I dispose of used oil and oil filters? Properly disposing of used oil is the legally and environmentally right action to take. Oil is considered hazardous waste and should never be poured on the ground or in storm drains. Please put the used oil in a closed container and take it, along with your used oil filter, to one of the locations provided below based on the county in which you reside. If you are a resident of Alamance County, you may take up to five gallons of used oil to the Alamance County landfill located at 2701 Austin Quarter Road in Graham. If you are a resident of Orange County, you may drop off used oil in containers at the Bradshaw Quarry Rd. Waste and Recycling Center located at 6705 Bradshaw Quarry Rd near Mebane. Used oil and filters are not collected by the city.\xa0 Some auto parts stores also collect these items if you purchased the oil and filter from the store. How do I properly dispose of paints and chemicals? The City of Mebane can not collect paints and chemicals. There are two Household Hazardous Waste collections in Orange County: The Eubanks Rd. Waste & Recycling Center The Walnut Grove Church Rd. Waste & Recycling Center Will the city collect my appliance? The City of Mebane will collect appliances, known as ‘white goods’, such as refrigerators, stoves, washers, dryers, and dishwashers. City staff will create a work order on your normal collection day to have your appliance collected. How do I find my recycling collection day? Click here to see the scheduled recycling collection map. How do I obtain a new garbage and/or recycling cart? For new residents to Mebane, you may pay for a new garbage cart when you start your city utility account at the Mebane City Hall located at 106 East Washington St. The fee for the monthly solid waste service and cart are shown in the city’s fee schedule. If you are moving into a home previously occupied, then a garbage and recycling cart should be at the residence. Once you pay for the new garbage cart, then the Public Works Department is notified of the purchase and a cart is delivered to your house along with a welcome packet of information. The garbage cart delivery will occur by the next business day after purchase. Since the city contracts recycling material hauling, the contractor will deliver your recycling cart typically the week after you established a city utility account. The recycling cart is paid to the contractor via the city’s contract, thus a Mebane resident doesn’t pay a recycling cart fee. My garbage cart is missing, what do I do? The garbage cart is purchased by the resident, and as such the resident is responsible for the cart. To aid in locating your cart, the cart is assigned to the address by the cart number located on the front of the container. Call the Public Works Department for your assigned cart number. Should you not be able to locate the cart, report the cart stolen to the Mebane Police Department. You may need to purchase a new cart if the cart isn’t located. Does the city offer solid waste collection for physically or elderly disabled citizens? The Public Works Department, Sanitation Division does offer special collection services upon receiving a valid request. Ask your doctor to complete a verification form which is available by calling the Public Works Department at 919-563-3401. Upon receiving the completed doctor’s verification form, special collection services will be scheduled for the duration agreed upon based on the doctor’s recommendation. This service is typically provided for the physically or elderly disabled. City staff will occasionally request an update from the receiver of this special service, typically via mail, to assure this service is still required. How do I properly dispose of extra cardboard? A cardboard convenience center is available for Mebane residents with excessive cardboard for recycling. The center is located in the parking lot of the Mebane Arts & Community Center at 633 Corregidor St. This center will only accept broken down (folded) cardboard boxes. Cardboard left outside the container will be taken to the landfill. Please properly dispose of trash. When do I place my garbage cart at the street for collection? Your garbage cart should be placed at the curb no later than 7:00 AM on your collection day. Remove your empty container from the curb by midnight on the day of collection. Carts should not be left on the curb during weekends. Your cooperation in abiding by this requirement helps keep the City of Mebane beautiful. On which day will my bulk item(s) be collected? Bulk items, such as mattresses, appliances, and furniture,\xa0 will be collected on your normal garbage collection day. You do not need to call in for a special collection unless you are requesting a large quantity of items to be disposed of and want city staff to review the amount to assure city collection of material. Please separate bulk items from your regular garbage as these items will be collected using a knuckle boom or rear loaded truck. The City does not collect debris from building or contract work, which includes tires, shingles, concrete, masonry, rocks, dirt, stumps, logs, hazardous chemicals, paint, or sealed containers.\xa0 These items should be disposed of in a complaint and appropriate manner by contacting the Alamance County landfill at (336)376-8902. On which day will my yard waste be collected? Yard waste will be collected on your normal garbage collection day. You do not need to call in for a special collection unless you are requesting a large quantity of yard waste to be disposed of and you want city staff to review the amount to assure city collection of material. The City will not pick up yard debris from contracted work. Yard waste is described as vegetative matter resulting from normal landscaping maintenance performed by the homeowner only, including but not limited to leaves, twigs, grass, limbs, shrub clippings, hay/pine straw, pine cones and small logs. City staff prefers that these items be placed in a permanent, reusable can or other receptacle to be provided by the customer. Disposable plastic bags or other disposable containers will be serviced, however, this option is not environmental friendly. State law requires yard waste to be separated from trash and recycling. Does Mebane offer loose leaf collection to its citizens? Seasonally, the City offers a loose-leaf collection program service for its citizens. The Sanitation Department collects loose leaves beginning in October and continues through early March each year. The program provides residents with the opportunity to have loose leaves collected at roadside without the need of bagging during this collection schedule. The program operates on a city-wide routing system that is based on city staff completing one leaf collection route of the entire city. Once the city route is completed, staff then returns to the beginning of the same planned route and begins again. A typical loose leaf collection route around the entire city takes about three to four weeks depending on the amount of leaves placed out for collection. This equates to about five complete loose leaf collection routes around the city. How do I request a 95-gallon recycling cart? If you currently have a 65-gallon recycling cart and would prefer a 95-gallon cart, please contact the Public Works Department at 919-563-3401 to request a cart change-out. Staff will submit your address to our recycling material collector, GFL Environmental, for changing out the\xa0cart. Then, simply leave your recycling cart at the street on your normal recycling collection day and GFL will change out the carts after collection occurred. Please note that GFL will remove the 65-gallon cart in place of the 95-gallon cart. Please allow one to two collection cycles for the cart change to occur and leave your cart at the street until at least 6:00 PM on your normal recycling collection day.', 'Cemetery Garbage and Recycling Collection Loose Leaf Collection Program Streets and Sidewalk City of Mebane Standards and Specifications Frequently Asked Questions Contact Public Works Staff Chuck Smith Public Works Director (919) 563-3401 (919) 304-6836 (FAX) Contact us Physical Address 636 Corregidor Street Mebane, NC 27302 Mailing Address 106 E Washington Street Mebane, NC 27302 Office Staff: Monday - Friday : 8:00 am - 5:00pm Operations: Monday - Friday : 7:00 am - 3:30 pm Saturday: Closed Sunday: Closed Public Works Frequently Asked Questions How do I find my garbage pickup day?
